{{Quote|Final automated log entry—recommended that this route be discontin—|The ''Zeta Five''{{'s}} message pod|<ref name="unknown" />}}
'''''Zeta Five''''' was a [[Cha'a drone ship]] owned by [[Cha'a]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Lord/Legends|lord]] [[Ssk Kahorr]], who used the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the ''Zeta Five'' was sent on a run to deliver supplies and components to a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] colony on [[Goluud Minor]], in the [[Primus Goluud system|Goluud system]]. Kahorr chose to send the ''Zeta Five'' along the [[Goluud Corridor]], a newly-discovered [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] that passed dangerously close to the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] unstable [[red]] giant [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]]. The ''Zeta Five'' reached the Goluud system without incident but arrived to find Primus Goluud in a violent phase of stellar activity. The ship was subsequently destroyed by a massive solar flare, although not before it jettisoned its [[message pod]], which had recorded the events regarding the ship's destruction.

==Description==
{{Quote|Tolerance beyond safety maximums—hull integrity failing.|The ''Zeta Five''{{'s}} message pod|<ref name="unknown" />}}
The ''Zeta Five'' was a [[Cha'a drone ship]] and was used as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was colored [[brown]] on the exterior, and on the top of the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] were three [[Solar sailer/Legends|solar sails]]. The ''Zeta Five'' also had three [[Engine/Legends|engines]] located on its back end. On the underside of the ship, ten apparatuses protruded from the ''Zeta Five'', five on each side. It did not require any [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings be aboard to [[Pilot/Legends|operate]] it, and the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] could calibrate [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps by itself. The log was recorded in a [[Message pod|Cha'a message pod]], which would be jettisoned for later recovery if the ship was damaged or destroyed.<ref name="unknown" />

==History==
{{Quote|Note, stellar activity in Primus Goluud has entered a violent phase…unexpected danger…|The ''Zeta Five''{{'s}} message pod|<ref name="unknown" />}}
The ''Zeta Five'' was owned by [[Ssk Kahorr]], a [[Cha'a]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Lord/Legends|lord]], as of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. The ''Zeta Five'' was usually docked in [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], where Kahorr based his operations. That year, Kahorr sent his ship to the [[Primus Goluud system|Goluud system]] to deliver components and supplies to a new [[Mining/Legends|mining]] colony on [[Goluud Minor]]. Kahorr chose to send the ''Zeta Five'' on the [[Goluud Corridor]], a recently-blazed [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] discovered by hyperspace explorers [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], because the route was shorter than other possible options.<ref name="unknown" />

Despite the dangers of sending a ship along the route, which crossed perilously close to the Goluud [[Star system/Legends|system's]] unstable [[red]] giant [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]], Kahorr sent the ''Zeta Five'' on its way. The ship reached the Goluud system without incident; however, by the time the ship reached the system, Primus Goluud had entered into a particularly violent stage of solar activity. The ship was annihilated by a large solar flare, but was able to jettison its message pod. The message pod was later recovered by Kahorr, revealing to him the fate of the ''Zeta Five''.<ref name="unknown" />

==Commanders and crew==
Like all drone ships, the ''Zeta Five'' did not require a sentient crew or [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. The ship was owned by Cha'a merchant lord Ssk Kahorr.<ref name="unknown" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Zeta Five'' appears in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. The ship makes its only appearance in the arc's first issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 1|''Into the Unknown'']], in which it is destroyed. In this appearance, it is illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="unknown" /> The ''Zeta Five'' is later mentioned, albeit indirectly, in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|first issue]] of ''The Golden Age of the Sith''{{'s}} follow-up arc, ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire]]''.<ref name="measures">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]]</ref>
